content: A physical quantity $\mathrm{P}=\frac{\mathrm{A}^3 \sqrt{\mathrm{~B}}}{\mathrm{C}^{-4} \mathrm{D}^{3 / 2}}$. Out of the quantities $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ and D , the quantity which brings maximum percentage error in P is
explanation:
In error analysis, the percentage error in a physical quantity depends on the power/exponent of each variable. Greater the power, more impact its error has on the overall expression.

Given:

$$ P=\frac{A^3 \sqrt{B}}{C^4 D^{3 / 2}} $$

Take percentage error:

$$ \frac{\Delta \mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{P}} \times 100=\left(3 \frac{\Delta \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~A}}+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\Delta \mathrm{~B}}{\mathrm{~B}}+4 \frac{\Delta \mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{C}}+\frac{3}{2} \frac{\Delta \mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{C}}\right) \times 100 $$

So, C has the highest power (4) $\rightarrow$ its error contributes most significantly to the percentage error in P .

content: A rigid body weighs 25 gram and has a measured volume of $5 \mathrm{~cm}^3$. The possible error in the measurement of mass and volume are 0.01 gram and $0.1 \mathrm{~cm}^3$. The maximum error in the density will be
explanation:
Given:

$ m = 25~\text{g}, \quad \Delta m = 0.01~\text{g} $

$ V = 5~\text{cm}^3, \quad \Delta V = 0.1~\text{cm}^3 $

We are to find maximum percentage error in density.

Step 1 – Formula for density:

$ \rho = \frac{m}{V} $

Step 2 – Error propagation (for maximum relative error):

$ \frac{\Delta\rho}{\rho} = \frac{\Delta m}{m} + \frac{\Delta V}{V} $

Step 3 – Substitute the values:

$ \frac{\Delta\rho}{\rho} = \frac{0.01}{25} + \frac{0.1}{5} $

Compute each term:

$ \frac{0.01}{25} = 0.0004 $

$ \frac{0.1}{5} = 0.02 $

Add them:

$ \frac{\Delta\rho}{\rho} = 0.0204 $

Step 4 – Convert to percentage:

$ \% \text{error in density} = 0.0204 \times 100 = 2.04\% $

✅ Final Answer:

Option D – 2.04 %
